traceRelations.direction: downstream -> upstream: the derived object is the source and points back to the origin it was derived from
traceRelations.inverse: gives-rise-to / basis-of (NOT a named member of the TraceRelation set)
traceRelations.note: General derivation edge. NOT constrained by any §25.1 invariant, so direction is INFERRED from usage. §29.2 traversal walks it downstream (Intent -> ... derived objects). Impact flows FORWARD onto the source when the target/origin is invalidated (source points at origin).

traceRelations.direction: more-specific/refining object -> coarser/refined object (source refines target)
traceRelations.note: INFERRED (not in §25.1). Backed by IntentObject.parentIntentId (§6.1: child intent refines parent). §29.2's 'Intent -> refines -> Requirement' is worded downstream for traversal, but the semantic source is the refining (more specific) object. No REQUIREMENT object type exists in this model; requirement-level refinement is carried by PWUs/obligations.

AUTHORITATIVE: §25.1 'DECOMPOSES must connect a parent PWU to a child PWU.' Mediated by DECOMPOSITION_CONTRACT (parentWorkUnitId + childWorkUnitIds, §13.1). Every child must trace to the parent (§13.2).

traceRelations.direction: satisfier (work product / completion claim) -> satisfied obligation/intent
traceRelations.note: INFERRED. Governed by §10.2: a child PWU may satisfy a parent obligation ONLY through explicit allocation; an obligation cannot become SATISFIED merely because a related PWU completed; satisfaction requires a SUPPORTED claim (§10.2, §15). Target is the obligation/constraint/intent-success-condition being fulfilled.
INFERRED. Backed by PWU.dependencyIds (§7.1) and DecompositionContract.siblingDependencyIds (§13.1). Failure of the target is invalidation trigger 'dependency failure' (§29.1) -> BLOCKED (§8.2).

INFERRED. Backed by PWU.constraintIds (§7.1) and Intent.constraintIds (§6.1). A mandatory-constraint change is invalidation trigger #2 (§29.1); impact flows FORWARD from the changed CONSTRAINT (target) back onto every object CONSTRAINED_BY it.

INFERRED. Backed by PWU.assumptionIds (§7.1) and AssumptionObject.affectedObjectIds (§12.1, the materialized inverse). Assumption FALSIFICATION/EXPIRY (trigger #3/#10) propagates along the inverse to every assuming object (§12.2, Scenario 2 §39).

INFERRED. Backed by §29.2 'PWU -> produces -> Artifact', PWU.expectedOutputs (§7.1) and ExecutionStep.outputBindings (§21). Note §16.2: generated prose is NOT automatically evidence, so PRODUCES(->ARTIFACT) does not imply an admissible EVIDENCE node.

AUTHORITATIVE: §25.1 'SUPPORTS must originate from Evidence or Assessment and target a Claim.' Backed by Evidence.supportsClaimIds (§16.1) and Claim.supportingEvidenceIds (§15.1). THE key edge for evidence-invalidation propagation. (§29.2's informal 'Artifact -> supports -> Evidence' link is traversal wording only; §25.1 restricts the true source set to Evidence/Assessment and target to Claim.)

INFERRED (parallel to SUPPORTS). Backed by Evidence.contradictsClaimIds (§16.1) and Claim.contradictingEvidenceIds (§15.1). Contradicting evidence must remain VISIBLE and is not erased by remediation (§15.2, §18.1).

AUTHORITATIVE on source: §25.1 'VERIFIES must originate from an Assessment.' Target set drawn from AssuranceAssessment.claimIds + subjectObjectIds (§18) and §29.2 'Claim -> verifies -> Baseline' (the assessment's verification underpins baseline readiness).

AUTHORITATIVE intent: §25.1 'Invalidated source objects may invalidate downstream trace claims.' This is an engine-EMITTED edge produced by impact analysis (§29), never a silent mutation. It is the machine-readable record of a §29.3 'invalidated' classification.

INFERRED but strongly grounded. Every object carries a SUPERSEDED status; Intent has supersedesIntentId (§6.1). THIS is the correction relation: history is append-oriented (§2.7), evidence corrections create a NEW version (§16.2), baseline changes create a successor (§24.2), and trace links cannot be silently rewritten (§25.1). Corrections emit a new object + SUPERSEDES link, never mutate in place.

AUTHORITATIVE: §25.1 'PROMOTES must connect a Decision to a Baseline or Baseline Item.' The Decision must be decisionType PROMOTE_BASELINE (§23.1) and EFFECTIVE; Baseline.promotionDecisionId is the inverse pointer (§24.1). Baseline promotion is a governance event, not an execution step (§24.2), requiring an explicit effective decision (§23.2).

traceRelations.direction: parent obligation (via decomposition contract) -> child PWU (allocatee)
traceRelations.note: INFERRED. Backed by §29.2 'Requirement -> allocated to -> PWU', DecompositionContract.obligationAllocations (§13.1), and the ObligationAllocated event (§26.3). §10.2: a child PWU may satisfy a parent obligation ONLY through explicit allocation. The 'No obligation disappears' property (§35.1) audits this edge: parent mandatory obligations = allocated + retained + satisfied + authorized waivers.

traceRelations.direction: parent-level constraint/assumption -> child PWU (or child constraint copy)
traceRelations.note: INFERRED. Backed by DecompositionContract.constraintPropagations + assumptionPropagations (§13.1) and ConstraintPropagated event (§26.3). Constraint propagation rule §11.2: every mandatory parent constraint must be propagated / retained / marked inapplicable / waived / superseded — 'No silent omission.' 'No constraint silently drops' property (§35.1) audits this edge.

INFERRED. Backed by §23 governance, AssurancePolicy.targetTypes (§17.1), and the authority model (§5). Authority REVOCATION is invalidation trigger #5 (§29.1) and 'Revocation triggers impact analysis' (§23.2) -> propagates along GOVERNS to every governed object (approvals, waivers, promotions).

invalidationRules.affects: Claims that are SUPPORTS-targets of that Evidence (Evidence -> Claim); AssuranceAssessments whose evidenceConsideredIds include it; and — via VERIFIES -> Baseline — baseline readiness.
invalidationRules.classification: invalidated | revalidation | needs-review — specifically: every dependent SUPPORTED claim must become CONTESTED, under-review (UNDER_ASSESSMENT), or INVALIDATED (never left silently SUPPORTED).
invalidationRules.propagation: Forward along SUPPORTS to each dependent Claim, then along VERIFIES to any Baseline. Claim.status OPEN->UNDER_ASSESSMENT or ->CONTESTED (§15.1). Assurance re-opens (revalidation) or requests fresh evidence (evidence-refresh). RECOMPUTE baseline-readiness: a CONTESTED claim cannot authorize baseline promotion unless resolved or waived (§15.2). If the evidence sits under an AUTHORITATIVE (immutable) baseline, the baseline is not mutated — a SUCCESSOR baseline is required (§24.2).
invalidationRules.sourceSection: §29.1 (evidence invalidation); §16.2 (Invalidated evidence cannot support an active claim; expiration triggers reassessment of dependent claims); §35.1 (Invalid evidence cannot support satisfaction: dependent supported claim -> contested/under-review/invalidated); §15.2; §24.2

invalidationRules.trigger: Intent REVISION (APPROVED -> REVISED, semantic version increments)
invalidationRules.affects: PWUs that REFINE / are DERIVED_FROM the intent and their downstream obligations, evidence, claims, and any baseline built on them.
invalidationRules.classification: needs-review or invalidated (affected SATISFIED PWUs become review-required or invalidated).
invalidationRules.propagation: Change-impact analysis is MANDATORY before proceeding (§6.3). Impact traverses affected PWUs and evidence (§29.2 chain). Prior baseline remains IMMUTABLE; a successor baseline is required (Scenario 5, §39).

invalidationRules.trigger: Mandatory CONSTRAINT change (add/alter mandatory constraint; semantic version = Yes per §4.1)
invalidationRules.affects: Objects CONSTRAINED_BY the constraint and every child it PROPAGATES to.
invalidationRules.classification: revalidation (re-assess conformance) escalating to reshaping/invalidated if the new constraint is now VIOLATED.
invalidationRules.propagation: Forward from the changed CONSTRAINT along CONSTRAINED_BY (and PROPAGATES to children). A VIOLATED constraint must affect assurance disposition; conformance claims re-enter assessment.

invalidationRules.affects: Objects in Assumption.affectedObjectIds (the ASSUMES inverse) — i.e., every PWU relying on it — and their evidence.
invalidationRules.classification: reshaping or replanning (controller selects reshape or replan); affected evidence -> needs-review; shape-integrity -> AT_RISK or VIOLATED. A falsified CRITICAL assumption may drive invalidated.
invalidationRules.propagation: Falsification triggers impact analysis (§12.2). During execution: EXECUTING -> RESHAPING (§8.2). Scenario 2: assumption FALSIFIED -> affected PWUs identified -> shapeIntegrityState AT_RISK/VIOLATED -> affected evidence reviewed -> controller selects reshape/replan -> execution does not continue blindly.

invalidationRules.affects: Decisions/approvals/waivers granted under that authority (via GOVERNS) and the objects those decisions authorized (PWUs, baselines, waived obligations).
invalidationRules.classification: needs-review or invalidated for governed objects; revoked approvals cascade.
invalidationRules.propagation: Revocation triggers impact analysis (§23.2). Human override / revocation must NOT erase prior assurance findings (§23.2). Waived obligations lose cover and revert (ACTIVE/VIOLATED); a baseline whose promotion decision is revoked -> Baseline REVOKED (§24.1).

invalidationRules.trigger: ASSURANCE-POLICY change (new policy semanticVersion; e.g., changed evidence requirement or criteria)
invalidationRules.affects: AssuranceAssessments performed under the prior policySemanticVersion and the claims they VERIFIED.
invalidationRules.classification: revalidation (assessments must be re-run under the new policy version); evidence-refresh if evidence requirements changed.
invalidationRules.propagation: Every assessment records policyId + policySemanticVersion (§18.1), so stale assessments are detectable and re-opened. Verified claims re-enter UNDER_ASSESSMENT.

invalidationRules.affects: Downstream PWUs / execution plans built on that architecture baseline and any dependent baselines.
invalidationRules.classification: replanning or reshaping for dependent PWUs; dependent baselines -> successor required (invalidated for readiness).
invalidationRules.propagation: An authoritative baseline is immutable; changes create a successor (§24.2); supersession preserves traceability. Impact flows to objects DERIVED_FROM / DEPENDS_ON the changed baseline.

invalidationRules.affects: PWUs / execution steps that DEPEND_ON the failed dependency.
invalidationRules.classification: replanning (BLOCKED, then change-tactic/replan); reshaping/invalidated if the dependency is permanently unavailable.
invalidationRules.propagation: PLANNED/EXECUTING -> BLOCKED on runtime dependency unavailable (§8.2). Step preconditions gate re-entry (§21.1).

invalidationRules.trigger: CHILD CONFLICT (sibling conflict discovered during/after recomposition)
invalidationRules.affects: The parent PWU's recomposition and conflicting sibling PWUs.
invalidationRules.classification: invalidated (RECOMPOSED + sibling conflict -> INVALIDATED) or reshaping; RecompositionContract -> CONFLICTED; siblings -> needs-review.
invalidationRules.propagation: Recomposition may FAIL even when every child PWU is individually satisfied (§14.1). RecompositionContract.status -> CONFLICTED/INSUFFICIENT (§14). Parent PWU RECOMPOSED -> INVALIDATED (§8.2).

invalidationRules.affects: Assumptions and Evidence keyed to external state (validUntil / expirationCondition) and the claims relying on them.
invalidationRules.classification: evidence-refresh and needs-review; assumptions -> EXPIRED (can no longer authorize work).
invalidationRules.propagation: Expired assumptions cannot continue authorizing work (§12.2). Evidence expiration must trigger reassessment of dependent claims (§16.2), routing dependent claims back through the evidence-invalidation rule above.

invalidationRules.trigger: IMPACT TRAVERSAL DIRECTION (engine behavior, all triggers)
invalidationRules.affects: Whatever lies along the typed TraceLink graph from the changed object.
invalidationRules.classification: n/a (mechanism) — produces one §29.3 classification per affected object.
invalidationRules.propagation: Traverse typed relationships: Intent -(refines)-> Requirement -(allocated to)-> PWU -(produces)-> Artifact -(supports)-> Evidence -(supports)-> Claim -(verifies)-> Baseline. Because TraceLinks are directed, impact flows FORWARD along SUPPORTS/VERIFIES/PROMOTES/PRODUCES/DECOMPOSES/ALLOCATES/PROPAGATES/GOVERNS/CONSTRAINED_BY-inverse, but REVERSE along DERIVED_FROM/REFINES/ASSUMES (which point upstream). Emit INVALIDATES / SUPERSEDES links for the record; never silently rewrite (§25.1).
invalidationRules.sourceSection: §29.2; §25.1; §32.5 (Change-Impact Projection: changed object, affected objects, invalidation status, required remediation)

invalidationRules.trigger: CONSERVATISM under high consequence (meta-rule over all triggers)
invalidationRules.affects: Any affected object whose PWU riskProfile has high consequence / irreversibility / security or regulatory exposure.
invalidationRules.classification: Bias UP the severity ladder: prefer invalidated/revalidation over needs-review when consequences are high.
invalidationRules.propagation: 'Automatic invalidation must be conservative where consequences are high' (§29.3). Ties to WorkRiskProfile (§9.2) and risk-proportional readiness. Chosen classification maps to a ControlAction (§37: RESHAPE_PWU, REPLAN_EXECUTION, REVISE_DECOMPOSITION, GATHER_EVIDENCE, ESCALATE, REJECT, WAIVE) and to AssuranceAssessment.recommendedControlAction (§18).

invalidationRules.trigger: CORRECTION = supersession, never mutation (invariant governing how invalidations are recorded)
invalidationRules.affects: The object being corrected + all objects tracing to its prior version.
invalidationRules.classification: Prior version -> SUPERSEDED; dependents re-classified per the relevant trigger rule.
invalidationRules.propagation: History is append-oriented (§2.7). A correction creates a NEW object version + a SUPERSEDES TraceLink (new -> old); evidence corrections create a new version (§16.2); baseline changes create a successor (§24.2). Trace links cannot be silently rewritten; invalidated source objects may invalidate downstream trace claims via emitted INVALIDATES edges (§25.1).

notes: CLOSED impact-classification set is EXACTLY the 7 outcomes enumerated in §29.3 (unaffected; review required; evidence refresh required; revalidation required; replanning required; reshaping required; invalidated). These match the client's parenthetical synonyms one-to-one. No other outcome values are defined in the document.

DISCREPANCY / GAP FLAG: The prompt says the canonical set is 'DOC-007's 18 values,' but the TraceRelation enum in THIS file (§25, lines 1391-1408) contains only 17: DERIVED_FROM, REFINES, DECOMPOSES, SATISFIES, DEPENDS_ON, CONSTRAINED_BY, ASSUMES, PRODUCES, SUPPORTS, CONTRADICTS, VERIFIES, INVALIDATES, SUPERSEDES, PROMOTES, ALLOCATES, PROPAGATES, GOVERNS. The 18th value cannot be recovered from this file — M6 must reconcile against DOC-007 directly (candidate missing edges by analogy: a REALIZES/TRACES_TO, or a split of ALLOCATES into ALLOCATES/ALLOCATED_TO). I did NOT fabricate an 18th.

AUTHORITATIVE vs INFERRED directionality: §25.1 hard-constrains only 4 relations — DECOMPOSES (parent PWU->child PWU), SUPPORTS (Evidence|Assessment->Claim), VERIFIES (from Assessment), PROMOTES (Decision->Baseline/Item). The other 13 source/target/direction assignments are INFERRED from object reference fields (§6-§24) and the §29.2 traversal, and are marked as such per-relation. Treat the 4 as invariants; treat the other 13 as strong-but-derived and confirm against DOC-007.

TraceLink is immutable and directed (§25.1: 'Trace links are directed and typed'; 'Trace links cannot be silently rewritten'). Corrections never mutate an edge or object in place — they emit a new object + a SUPERSEDES link (new->old) and, where downstream validity is affected, engine-emitted INVALIDATES links (source->dependent). SUPERSEDES and INVALIDATES are the two engine-authored relations; the other 15 are authored during normal work.

Object-state realization of each classification (for M6 to map onto): 'invalidated' -> PWU.workLifecycleState=INVALIDATED (§7.2) / AssuranceState=INVALIDATED (§7.4) / Evidence.status=INVALIDATED (§16.1) / Constraint.status=INVALIDATED (§11.1); 'reshaping required' -> workLifecycleState=RESHAPING + shapeIntegrityState=RESHAPING_REQUIRED (§7.2,§7.5); 'evidence refresh required' -> AssuranceState=EVIDENCE_REQUIRED + ControlAction GATHER_EVIDENCE (§7.4,§37); 'revalidation required' -> AssuranceState back to READY_FOR_ASSESSMENT/ASSESSING + Claim.status=UNDER_ASSESSMENT; 'replanning required' -> ControlAction REPLAN_EXECUTION/CHANGE_TACTIC + PWU BLOCKED->re-PLANNED (§8.2,§37); 'review required' has NO dedicated lifecycle literal — realize via CHALLENGED / Claim.UNDER_ASSESSMENT / an open AssuranceObservation (Scenario 5 uses the phrase 'review-required', §39).

Baseline-readiness recompute (the emphasized evidence case) is bounded by two invariants: §15.2 'A contested claim cannot authorize baseline promotion unless resolved or waived' and §35.1 'No unauthorized baseline' + 'Invalid evidence cannot support satisfaction.' Because an AUTHORITATIVE baseline is immutable (§24.2), invalidation never edits a promoted baseline — it forces a SUCCESSOR baseline (Scenario 5, §39).

The Change-Impact Projection (§32.5) is the read model this engine feeds: {changed object, affected objects, invalidation status, required remediation}. Chosen classifications map to ControlActions in §37 and to AssuranceAssessment.recommendedControlAction (§18): RESHAPE_PWU, REVISE_DECOMPOSITION, REPLAN_EXECUTION, GATHER_EVIDENCE, ESCALATE, WAIVE, REJECT, ACCEPT.
